definition:
1. to remove to  a place, plant, settle  or establish  there as colonists , κ. τινὰ εἰς τόπον (Herdotus Historicus); γυναῖκας ἐς φῶς ἡλίου κατ. (Euripides):—;also, κ. τινὰ ἐν τόπῳ to settle  or plant  one in , (Sophocles Tragicus); ἐλπίδας ἔν τινι κ. to plant  them in  his mind, (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
2. with accusative loci, to colonise, people  a place, (Herdotus Historicus), etc. 
3. Pass., 
4. of persons, to be placed  or settled , ἐν τόπῳ (Herdotus Historicus); ἐς τόπον (Thucydides) 
5. of places, to have colonies planted there, to be colonised , (Thucydides) 
6. to bring home and re-establish there, to restore to one's country , (Aeschulus Tragicus) (ML)
